source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
The picture on the right has been drawn on paper and cut out to make a house. Which of the houses does it make?
<image1>
<image2>

issues: Option texts are not available; metadata states the options field contains only option labels.

image_dependency: Question text explicitly asks about a pictured cut-out and which house it makes, so the visual content is necessary.
Asset registry includes an image file and registry_passed=true, indicating image assets are part of the sample.
There is some asset ambiguity because the registry issues note two image placeholders in the question context but only one explicit image path.
multi_step: The task appears to require spatial reasoning from a cut-out/net to a resulting house shape, not simple text lookup.
The question then requires comparing the inferred result against multiple choices (5 options).
This suggests at least two reasoning stages: interpret the pictured cut-out, then match it to an answer option.
Score is kept moderate because option texts are unavailable and the image reference mismatch adds uncertainty about the exact reasoning depth.
verifiability: answer_type is option/choice and answer_text is a single label ('A'), so final-answer checking is straightforward.
choices contain five labeled options, making answer matching well-defined at the label level.
Verifiability is reduced somewhat because option contents are not available and the registry notes a mismatch between referenced image placeholders and provided image paths.
